senses.definition: Able to remember information easily and keep it in your memory for a long time.
examples: She has a very retentive memory for names and faces.
A retentive mind is useful when you study foreign languages.

senses.definition: Able to hold or keep something such as water, heat, or air without letting it escape easily.
examples: The soil here is very retentive and stays wet after heavy rain.
This material is highly retentive of heat.

senses.definition: In a way that shows the ability to remember or keep information easily.
examples: Children often learn new words retentively at that age.
He listened retentively and could repeat the lecture later.

senses.definition: The quality of being able to remember information easily and keep it in your memory.
examples: Her retentiveness makes her an excellent student.
Memory retentiveness decreases with age for many people.

senses.definition: The quality of holding or keeping something such as water, heat, or air.
examples: The clay's retentiveness of moisture helps the plants survive dry periods.
High heat retentiveness is important in cooking pans.

senses.definition: Plural of retentiveness; different kinds or instances of the ability to retain or remember things, or to hold substances.
examples: The study compared the retentivenesses of different soil types.
Various memory retentivenesses were observed among the participants.

senses.definition: Plural of retentive, especially used for things that hold substances such as moisture or heat.
examples: These clays are excellent retentives of water.
The materials differ in how powerful retentives of heat they are.

senses.definition: Plural of retentivity; different degrees or types of ability to retain something such as moisture, heat, or magnetism.
examples: The experiment measured the retentivities of several alloys.
Different soils show different moisture retentivities.

senses.definition: The ability of a material to keep something such as moisture, heat, or magnetism and not lose it quickly.
examples: This soil has high water retentivity.
The retentivity of the magnet decreases over time.

senses.definition: Something that retains or holds back another thing, especially in technical or medical use.
examples: The device acts as a retentor for the joint.
A fluid retentor was installed in the system.

senses.definition: Money that is held back from a payment, especially in French legal or financial use.
examples: A small retenue was kept until the work was finished.
The contract allowed a retenue on each invoice.

senses.definition: A genus of bryozoans, small aquatic animals that live in colonies, known from fossils.
examples: Fossils of Retepora are found in many marine rocks.
The genus Retepora is important in paleontological studies.

senses.definition: A bryozoan of the genus Retepora or a related net-like fossil form.
examples: The limestone contains many retepore fragments.
Each retepore shows a delicate net structure.

senses.definition: A family of bryozoans that includes the genus Retepora.
examples: Reteoporidae are common in some fossil reef systems.
The family Reteoporidae shows complex colony forms.

senses.definition: To test someone or something again to check the result or to see if there is any change.
examples: The teacher decided to retest the class next week.
The car was retested after the repairs.

senses.definition: A second test that is given to someone or something.
examples: She passed the retest with a much higher score.
If you fail the exam, you can take a retest.

senses.definition: Past simple and past participle of retest.
examples: The machine was retested after every adjustment.
They retested the water for contamination.

senses.definition: Past simple and past participle of retestify; gave testimony again, especially in a legal setting.
examples: The witness retestified after new evidence appeared.
He retestified to clarify his earlier statement.

senses.definition: Third person singular of retestify; gives testimony again, especially in a legal setting.
examples: If asked, she retestifies to the same facts.
The expert retestifies when the case returns to court.

senses.definition: To give testimony again, especially in a court or official hearing.
examples: The witness may have to retestify if the trial is reopened.
He agreed to retestify before the committee.

senses.definition: Present participle of retestify; giving testimony again, especially in a legal setting.
examples: By retestifying, she corrected her earlier mistake.
He is retestifying before the court this afternoon.

senses.definition: Plural of retestimony; additional sets of statements given again under oath.
examples: The court records include several retestimonies.
Their retestimonies confirmed the earlier evidence.

senses.definition: A second or later set of statements given under oath, usually to clarify or repeat earlier testimony.
examples: Her retestimony was shorter but more precise.
The judge ordered a retestimony from the key witness.

senses.definition: Present participle of retest; testing again to check results or changes.
examples: They are retesting the samples for accuracy.
Retesting the students gave more reliable data.

senses.definition: The action or process of testing again.
examples: Regular retesting is required for safety.
The retesting of equipment happens every year.

senses.definition: Third person singular of retest; tests again.
examples: The lab routinely retests all abnormal results.
The teacher retests the class after a review.

senses.definition: Plural of retest; several second tests.
examples: The school offers free retests for failed exams.
Many retests were needed after the new policy.

senses.definition: To change the texture of a surface, material, or digital image.
examples: The designer decided to retexture the walls for a softer feel.
They retextured the game characters to make them more realistic.

senses.definition: Past simple and past participle of retexture.
examples: They retextured the ceiling to hide the cracks.
The model was completely retextured for the new game.

senses.definition: Having had its surface or appearance changed.
examples: The retextured walls gave the room a warmer look.
She chose a retextured fabric for the sofa.

senses.definition: Third person singular of retexture; changes the texture of something.
examples: The artist retextures old photos for a vintage effect.
The software automatically retextures the model.

senses.definition: Plural of retexture; different versions with changed surfaces or appearances, especially in digital art or games.
examples: The game includes several retextures of the same character.
Fans share their own retextures online.

senses.definition: Present participle of retexture; changing the texture of something.
examples: They are retexturing the road surface to reduce noise.
He spent hours retexturing the 3D model.

senses.definition: The process of changing the surface or appearance of something.
examples: Retexturing can completely change the feel of a room.
The update focused on lighting and retexturing.

senses.definition: To thank someone again.
examples: I must rethank you for all your help.
She rethanked him in her closing remarks.

senses.definition: To put new thatch on a roof again.
examples: They had to rethatch the cottage after the storm.
The old inn is rethatched every twenty years.

senses.definition: To thaw something again after it has been frozen once more.
examples: Do not rethaw meat that has already been thawed once.
The ice cream was rethawed and lost its texture.

senses.definition: An archaic form of "those".
examples: He spoke of rethe days long past.
Rethe tales are hard to believe.

senses.definition: The quality or state of being that or those, used playfully or creatively in modern writing.
examples: The film captures the pure retheness of teenage life.
He wrote about the city's strange retheness at night.

senses.definition: The act of creating a new theory or new explanation of something, often in academic work.
examples: The book offers a retheorization of nationalism.
Her article is a bold retheorization of classic economic ideas.

senses.definition: Plural of retheorization; different new theoretical explanations of something.
examples: Recent retheorizations of gender have changed the field.
The book reviews several retheorizations of democracy.

senses.definition: To create a new theory or explanation of something, usually in academic or intellectual work.
examples: The author attempts to retheorize globalization.
They retheorized the problem using recent data.

senses.definition: Past simple and past participle of retheorize.
examples: The phenomenon was retheorized in light of new evidence.
She retheorized the role of the state in her later work.

senses.definition: Third person singular of retheorize; creates a new theory or explanation of something.
examples: The article retheorizes power as a network.
Her study retheorizes identity in a useful way.

senses.definition: Present participle of retheorize; creating a new theory or explanation of something.
examples: They are retheorizing the relationship between work and leisure.
By retheorizing the data, she found a new pattern.

senses.definition: The process of making new theories or explanations about something.
examples: Rethinking and retheorizing are central to critical studies.
The book is an exercise in retheorizing.

senses.definition: A dialect or archaic spelling of "rather".
examples: I would rether stay at home.
He seemed rether tired that evening.

senses.definition: To make something thick again after it has become thinner or more liquid.
examples: You can rethicken the sauce by simmering it longer.
The mixture was rethickened with more flour.

senses.definition: To think again carefully about a plan, idea, or system, especially to change or improve it.
examples: We need to rethink our marketing strategy.
After the accident, she rethought her priorities.

senses.definition: An act of thinking again about something in order to change or improve it.
examples: The policy needs a complete rethink.
There has been a major rethink of how we use energy.

senses.definition: A person who rethinks things and often questions existing ideas or systems.
examples: She is known as a bold rethinker of traditional theory.
The conference brought together rethinking thinkers and rethinkers.

senses.definition: Plural of rethinker; people who rethink things and often question existing ideas or systems.
examples: Rethinkers in the field have challenged old models.
The company hired several rethinkers to improve its processes.

senses.definition: Present participle of rethink; thinking again carefully about something, especially to change it.
examples: We are rethinking how we deliver our courses.
Rethinking the plan saved the project.

senses.definition: The process of thinking again about something in order to improve it.
examples: There is a general rethinking of education happening now.
The book calls for a rethinking of our values.

senses.definition: Third person singular of rethink; thinks again carefully about something, especially to change it.
examples: The company rethinks its goals every year.
She often rethinks her decisions.

senses.definition: Plural of rethink; several acts of thinking again about something.
examples: There have been many rethinks of the project.
Policy rethinks are common after an election.

senses.definition: Past simple and past participle of rethink.
examples: They rethought their approach after the failure.
I have completely rethought my career plans.

senses.definition: To discuss or argue about something again, often in a way that repeats old points.
examples: I don't want to rethrash that argument.
The meeting rethrashed issues we had already settled.

senses.definition: To put a thread through something again, such as a needle, hole, or machine.
examples: You need to rethread the needle after it slipped out.
She stopped to rethread the sewing machine.

senses.definition: To put something back through a path or route that it followed before.
examples: The cable had to be rethreaded through the wall.
They rethreaded the strap through the buckle.

senses.definition: Past simple and past participle of rethread.
examples: She rethreaded the needle quickly.
They rethreaded the cable through the conduit.

senses.definition: Having had the thread, cable, or similar part put back through it.
examples: The rethreaded machine ran smoothly.
She handed him the rethreaded needle.

senses.definition: Present participle of rethread; putting a thread, cable, or similar item through something again.
examples: She sat by the window, rethreading the needle.
Rethreading the cable took longer than expected.

senses.definition: The act of putting thread, cable, or a similar item through something again.
examples: The repair required some careful rethreading.
Rethreading is necessary whenever the line breaks.

senses.definition: Third person singular of rethread; puts thread, cable, or something similar through something again.
examples: She patiently rethreads the machine whenever it jams.
He rethreads the cable after each test.

senses.definition: Plural of rethread; acts or instances of putting thread or something similar through again.
examples: Several rethreads were needed during the project.
Frequent rethreads can damage the material.
